observations.correspondence_rationale: The text references an old story warning not to look back at a place that has chosen to destroy itself, directly alluding to the biblical account of Lot's wife turning into a pillar of salt when she looked back at Sodom's destruction in Genesis 19:26.
observations.summary: The narrative alludes to the biblical story of Lot's wife, emphasizing the peril of looking back at a doomed place, symbolizing judgment and irreversible destruction.

evidence.quote: Some old stories warn you not to look back at such a place when you leave it.

evidence.quote: Some old stories warn you not to look back at such a place when you leave it.
